platform: custom gay tube (Cloudflare-protected, no WordPress indicators)

search: https://manporn.xxx/search/?q={query}  -- likely Cloudflare-gated for headless requests

metadata_mapping.scene_title: JSON-LD VideoObject > name  (also og:title, also <title> minus ' - manporn.xxx' or ' | ManPorn.XXX')
metadata_mapping.date: JSON-LD VideoObject > uploadDate (ISO-8601, strip to YYYY-MM-DD)
metadata_mapping.description: JSON-LD VideoObject > description  (may be comma-separated tag list on user-uploaded content; narrative on studio content)
metadata_mapping.cover_image: JSON-LD VideoObject > thumbnailUrl[0]  (CDN full-size thumb URL)
metadata_mapping.performers: JSON-LD VideoObject > actor[].name  (also a[href^='/models/'] anchor text in page body)
metadata_mapping.tags: JSON-LD VideoObject > keywords[]  (also a[href^='/categories/'] or a[href^='/tags/'] anchor text in page body)
metadata_mapping.studio: Constant 'ManPorn' — no reliable per-scene channel/studio field in JSON-LD

scene_title: Fallback: <title> tag, strip ' - manporn.xxx' or ' | ManPorn.XXX' suffix
date: Expected format: '2024-05-01T00:00:00+00:00' — split on T and take date part
description: Note: may be a comma-separated tag list on user-uploaded content, not a narrative description
cover_image: Fallback: og:image meta tag (typically the poster/thumb CDN URL)
performers: Parse JSON-LD VideoObject: json_ld['actor'] list -> each item has 'name' and 'url'
Fallback: CSS selector 'a[href^="/models/"]' anchor text from page HTML (filter out nav/static links)
tags: Fallback: CSS selector 'a[href^="/categories/"]' or 'a[href^="/tags/"]' anchor text in page HTML

search_strategy.notes: https://manporn.xxx/search/?q={query} exists but is expected to return a Cloudflare JS challenge for headless/script requests, consistent with the pattern seen on comparable gay tube sites (boyfriendtv.com, gay0day.com). sceneByName, sceneByQueryFragment, and sceneByFragment are therefore not reliably implementable without a JS-capable browser (e.g. playwright/selenium). Recommend disabling or documenting as unsupported until a workaround is found.

fragment_matching_strategy.notes: Depends on search endpoint, which is expected to be Cloudflare-blocked for script requests.

anti_bot_or_access_notes: Cloudflare protection is active on the site. Age-gate may also be present.

Scene detail pages (/videos/{id}/{slug}/) are expected to be accessible with a realistic Firefox/Linux UA, correct Accept and Accept-Language headers, and an age-gate cookie (e.g. 'age_verified=1' or site-specific equivalent — confirm on first fetch).
Python urllib and curl subprocess fallback should both work for scene pages.
Performer pages (/models/{slug}/) and search pages (/search/?q=) may trigger Cloudflare JS challenge — do not depend on live fetches for these without a JS-capable workaround.

Required headers: 'User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:109.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/115.0', 'Accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8', 'Accept-Language: en-US,en;q=0.5', 'Referer: https://manporn.xxx/'
Required cookie: 'age_verified=1' (name may differ — check response cookies on first fetch and adjust if needed)
known_limitations: sceneByName, sceneByQueryFragment, and sceneByFragment are not supported — search is expected to be Cloudflare-gated.
performerByURL may be blocked by Cloudflare — performer pages may trigger JS challenge. Return name-from-slug + gender constant ('Male') as fallback.
Scene description may be a comma-separated tag list on user-uploaded content, not a narrative synopsis.
No reliable per-scene studio/channel field — studio is always constant 'ManPorn'.
Age-gate cookie name must be confirmed on first fetch (expected 'age_verified=1' by analogy with similar sites; adjust if response sets a different cookie).
